purpose: Estimates systematic Fed policy rate bias from AI-related productivity mismeasurement
components: BLS service sector quality adjustment gaps
Intangible capital undercounting in GDP/productivity statistics
1990s revision precedent (1.0-1.5pp upward productivity revision after the fact)
AI Solow paradox window: 2-4 year period of maximum policy error risk
current_assessment: If AI is genuinely boosting productivity but measurement fails to capture it, the Taylor rule output gap estimate is too pessimistic and the implied policy rate is too low — risking inflation. Conversely, if AI is not boosting productivity, current pricing is correct. The Solow paradox window creates maximum uncertainty.
limitations: Mismeasurement magnitude is itself unmeasurable in real time
1990s revision precedent may not apply — different statistical methodology now
Four indistinguishable data interpretations require opposite policy responses
The 50-100bp bias estimate is highly model-dependent
